stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • A small number of US Navy sailors of Pacific Islander descent served during the Spanish-American War (1898).
  • Henry Kaleo was born in Hawaii around 1862.
  • He enlisted in the US Navy in March 1898.
  • Joseph Maile was born in Hawaii around 1859.
  • He enlisted in the US Navy in October 1894.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the historical significance of Henry Kaleo's enlistment date. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Both Henry Kaleo and Joseph Maile were US Navy sailors of Pacific Islander descent, but Kaleo enlisted in 1898, four years later than Maile.
Henry Kaleo enlisted in the US Navy in March 1898, making him one of the few sailors of Pacific Islander descent to serve in the Spanish-American War of 1898.
Henry Kaleo was a US Navy sailor of Pacific Islander descent, along with Joseph Maile, who was born in Hawaii around 1859.
When Henry Kaleo enlisted in the US Navy in March 1898, he joined sailors such as Joseph Maile, who had been born in Hawaii around 1859.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it pinpoints the historical significance of the 1898 enlistment date: it placed Kaleo among the few Pacific Islander sailors serving in the Spanish-American War.

Choices A, C, and D compare Kaleo to Maile without highlighting the significance of the enlistment date.
